view.container.title: Japanese Novel
view.books.name: Books
viewsWelcome.books.content:
No books yet.
A `.jpbook` collects your chapters into one book — create one and save it in your workspace folder.
[Create a Book File](command:workbench.action.files.newUntitledFile?%7B%22languageId%22%3A%22novel-jp-book%22%7D)
[Open the Guide](command:jpnov.openGuide)
viewsWelcome.books.noFolder.content:
Book files (`.jpbook`) live inside a workspace folder, but none is open.
Open your novel's folder first, then create the book file there — the guide walks through it.
[Open Folder](command:workbench.action.files.openFolder)
[Open the Guide](command:jpnov.openGuide)
walkthrough.title: Get started with Japanese Novel
walkthrough.description: Write, preview, and build a vertical-writing Japanese novel.
walkthrough.chapter.title: Write your first chapter
walkthrough.chapter.description:
Chapters are plain `.jpnov` files. Create one and start writing — ruby, emphasis dots, and page breaks are Aozora Bunko annotations, highlighted as you type. Save it with a `.jpnov` name to build a book from it later.
[Create a Chapter](command:workbench.action.files.newUntitledFile?%7B%22languageId%22%3A%22novel-jp%22%7D)
walkthrough.preview.title: See it laid out vertically
walkthrough.preview.description:
Open the live preview to see your manuscript in vertical, right-to-left genkō-yōshi layout. It updates as you write.
[Open the Preview](command:jpnov.previewToSide)
walkthrough.build.title: Collect chapters and build your book
walkthrough.build.description:
A `.jpbook` collects one book's chapters — one file per line, in reading order. It works inside an opened folder (your workspace) — if you haven't opened your novel's folder yet, [open it first](command:workbench.action.files.openFolder). Save the book file (and your chapters) anywhere inside; each path counts from the workspace folder itself, so moving the `.jpbook` never breaks them. An optional `---`-fenced block at the top carries the book's own metadata: `title`, the page header (`header` — e.g. the volume name), and the page-number style. Then open the Japanese Novel panel, tick the books you want, and build them to paginated HTML, a print-ready PDF, or Aozora-format text from the title-bar actions.
[Create a Book File](command:workbench.action.files.newUntitledFile?%7B%22languageId%22%3A%22novel-jp-book%22%7D)
[Open the Books Panel](command:workbench.view.extension.jpnov)
walkthrough.highlight.title: Optional: writing and proofing aids
walkthrough.highlight.description:
Two optional aids. Add your cast and coined words so they light up in the narration as you write — a name is coloured where it reads as the subject (`巳一は`), keywords are bolded everywhere. And turn on the publication-style checks — general novel style, no mixed periods — to catch manuscript-convention slips before you submit.
[Open Highlight Settings](command:workbench.action.openSettings?%22jpnov.highlight%22)
[Open Lint Settings](command:workbench.action.openSettings?%22jpnov.lint%22)
jpnov.lint.title: Japanese Novel — Lint
jpnov.lint.common.sentenceLength.descrip: Warn when a sentence is longer than this many characters. Recommended: 100. Leave empty to disable.
jpnov.lint.common.maxTen.description: Warn when a sentence has more than this many commas (、). Recommended: 3. Leave empty to disable.
jpnov.lint.common.maxKanjiRun.descriptio: Warn when this many kanji appear in a row. Recommended: 6. Leave empty to disable.
jpnov.lint.common.noEmDash.description: Flag a single em dash (—); novels use a double dash (――).
jpnov.lint.common.noNfd.description: Flag decomposed (NFD) characters; prefer the composed (NFC) form.
jpnov.lint.common.noZeroWidth.descriptio: Flag zero-width spaces and similar invisible characters.
jpnov.lint.common.jaNoSpaceBetweenFullWi: Flag a half-width space between two full-width characters. Auto-fixable.
jpnov.lint.common.jaUnnaturalAlphabet.de: Flag unnatural runs of half-width letters (likely typos).
jpnov.lint.common.minusPosition.descript: Flag a minus sign that is not directly before a number.
jpnov.lint.narration.generalNovelStyle.d: Check general Japanese novel conventions as one bundle: paragraph indent (　 or an opening bracket), a space after ！ / ？, even-count runs of … and ―, Arabic numerals at most two digits, and more. Auto-fixable.
jpnov.lint.narration.jaNoMixedPeriod.des: Flag a narration sentence that does not end with 。 (― and … are allowed). Auto-fixable.
jpnov.lint.ruby.kana.description: Require every ruby reading 《…》 to be all one kana type (this also rejects half-width kana and NFD). Left-ruby readings are not checked — they are often Latin.
jpnov.lint.ruby.kana.off.label: Don't check
jpnov.lint.ruby.kana.off.description: No kana-type check on ruby readings.
jpnov.lint.ruby.kana.hiragana.label: All hiragana
jpnov.lint.ruby.kana.hiragana.descriptio: Every reading must be hiragana only.
jpnov.lint.ruby.kana.katakana.label: All katakana
jpnov.lint.ruby.kana.katakana.descriptio: Every reading must be katakana only.
jpnov.layout.title: Japanese Novel — Layout
jpnov.preview.title: Japanese Novel — Preview
jpnov.html.title: Japanese Novel — HTML Output
jpnov.layout.charsPerLine.description: Characters per line for the preview and the HTML build grid. Default: 40 (a standard manuscript-submission width).
jpnov.layout.linesPerPage.description: Lines per page for the HTML build grid. The preview is a continuous flow and ignores this. Default: 34 (40×34 is a common novel manuscript-submission format).
jpnov.layout.kinsoku.description: Japanese line-breaking rules: keep forbidden characters off line starts and ends, never split ―― or ……, and hang a trailing 、。 into the margin. Applies to both the preview and the built book.
jpnov.layout.kinsoku.none.label: Off
jpnov.layout.kinsoku.none.description: No line-breaking rules — bare wrap at the column width.
jpnov.layout.kinsoku.normal.label: Normal
jpnov.layout.kinsoku.normal.description: Novel-grade rules: brackets, punctuation, small kana, ー, 々.
jpnov.layout.kinsoku.strict.label: Strict
jpnov.layout.kinsoku.strict.description: Everything in Normal, plus middle dots (・：；), repetition marks, and unbreakable symbol runs.
jpnov.layout.autoTateChuYoko.description: Automatically set half-width punctuation pairs upright in one square (tate-chu-yoko / 縦中横). Manual ［＃縦中横］…［＃縦中横終わり］ always applies regardless of this setting. Reflected in the preview and both built books; the text build writes explicit markers. Applies to every matching pair with no per-instance opt-out — use full-width marks to keep a pair as-is.
jpnov.layout.autoTateChuYoko.none.label: Off
jpnov.layout.autoTateChuYoko.none.descri: Half-width pairs stay as typed, lying on their side.
jpnov.layout.autoTateChuYoko.punctuation: A pair of half-width ! / ? stands upright in one square.
jpnov.preview.lineNumbers.description: Show a small line number in the head margin of each line in the preview.
jpnov.preview.edgeLine.description: Draw a ruled line between lines (like manuscript paper) in the preview.
jpnov.preview.edgeLine.none.label: None
jpnov.preview.edgeLine.text.label: Text color
jpnov.preview.edgeLine.text.description: Ruled lines in the text color.
jpnov.preview.edgeLine.red.label: Red
jpnov.preview.edgeLine.red.description: Red ruled lines, like classic manuscript paper.
jpnov.html.lineNumbers.description: Show a small line number in the head margin of each line in the built HTML.
jpnov.html.edgeLine.description: Draw a ruled line between lines and a matching border around each page in the built HTML.
jpnov.html.edgeLine.none.label: None
jpnov.html.edgeLine.text.label: Text color
jpnov.html.edgeLine.text.description: Ruled lines in the text color.
jpnov.html.edgeLine.red.label: Red
jpnov.html.edgeLine.red.description: Red ruled lines, like classic manuscript paper.
jpnov.project.title: Japanese Novel — Project
jpnov.project.outDir.description: Folder (relative to the workspace folder) where built `.html` / `.txt` books are written. This folder is never scanned for `.jpbook` books. Default: `dist`.
jpnov.build.title: Japanese Novel — PDF Output
jpnov.build.browserPath.description: Path to a Chrome, Edge, Chromium, or Brave executable used to convert the built HTML to PDF (Build to PDF). Leave empty to auto-detect an installed browser. The `CHROME_PATH` and `PUPPETEER_EXECUTABLE_PATH` environment variables are also honored. macOS example: `/Applications/Google Chrome.app/Contents/MacOS/Google Chrome`.
jpnov.book.title: Japanese Novel — Book Files
jpnov.book.updateReferencesOnFileMove.de: When a chapter file or folder is renamed or moved inside VS Code, update the paths listed in the workspace's book files (`.jpbook`). Renames made outside VS Code can't be tracked — a missing path is flagged in the editor instead.
jpnov.highlight.title: Japanese Novel — Highlighting
jpnov.highlight.characters.description: Your cast, one name per item (e.g. `朝霧　巳一`). A name is split on the half-/full-width space into surname + given, and the full name, the surname alone, and the given name alone are each recognised. Highlighted only in narration where it reads as a subject — the name (one honorific such as `さん` / `先生` allowed) directly followed by `は` or `が`. Dialogue is never coloured. Empty and duplicate items are ignored.
jpnov.highlight.keywords.description: Coined terms — a fantasy noun, a place name, … — one per item. Bolded wherever they appear in narration, without changing colour. Where a term is also a character, the character subject wins (`境無は` is the character; a bare `境無` is a keyword). Empty and duplicate items are ignored.
